title: Church of St. Anne, Talaulim
tagline: Church of St. Anne, Talaulim — spiritual town of Goa

heroImage.alt: Church of St. Anne, Talaulim, Goa

overview.short: Church of St. Anne, Talaulim is a much-loved spiritual town in Goa, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: The Church of St. Anne is a church and religious monument located in the Santana district of Old Goa, in Goa province, India. The 17th century church is a major example of the colonial Portuguese Baroque architecture built in Portuguese India.

topPlaces.name: Chimbel
topPlaces.description: Chimbel is a census town in North Goa district in the state of Goa, India.

topPlaces.name: Kendriya Vidyalaya Bambolim
topPlaces.description: K V Bambolim is a Kendriya Vidyalaya in the 3TTR, 5TTR and 2STC army camp, in Bambolim, Goa, India. Nested in the mountains of Bambolim, it has a panoramic view of the city of Panaji.

topPlaces.name: Church of St. Augustine, Goa
topPlaces.description: Church of St. Augustine is a ruined church complex located in Old Goa.

topPlaces.name: Museum of Christian Art
topPlaces.description: The Museum of Christian Art is an Indian museum of Christian art, located in the Convent of Santa Monica in the former colonial capital of Old Goa, Goa.

topPlaces.name: Batim
topPlaces.description: Batim is a village situated in the Tiswadi taluka of North Goa district. The village received widespread publicity in 1993 due to an alleged apparition of the Virgin Mary.

topPlaces.name: GMC Athletic Stadium
topPlaces.description: The GMC Athletic Stadium in Bambolim is the first integrated athletic complex in Goa. It has been constructed according to the IAAF specifications and norms and has hosted the athletic events of 2014 Lusofonia Games.

topPlaces.name: Basilica of Bom Jesus
topPlaces.description: The Basilica of Bom Jesus (Portuguese: Basílica do Bom Jesus; Goan Konkani: Borea Jezuchi Bajilika) is a Catholic basilica in Goa, in the Konkan region of India. The church is a pilgrimage centre and recognised by UNESCO as a World Heritage Site.

topPlaces.name: Pelourinho Novo
topPlaces.description: The Pelourinho Novo (lit. 'New Pillory'), also known as Hath Katro Khamb (lit. 'Hand-Cutting Pillar'), is a sixteenth-century black basalt pillory in Old Goa, located in the Indian coastal state of Goa. It acted as a symbolic civic marker and a focal point for official proceedings, where authorities handled legal matters, issued formal announcements, and enforced penalties, including corporal punishment such as the whipping of offenders.

K V Bambolim is a Kendriya Vidyalaya in the 3TTR, 5TTR and 2STC army camp, in Bambolim, Goa, India. Nested in the mountains of Bambolim, it has a panoramic view of the city of Panaji.
Church of St. Augustine is a ruined church complex located in Old Goa.

items.note: The Museum of Christian Art is an Indian museum of Christian art, located in the Convent of Santa Monica in the former colonial capital of Old Goa, Goa.
Batim is a village situated in the Tiswadi taluka of North Goa district. The village received widespread publicity in 1993 due to an alleged apparition of the Virgin Mary.

The GMC Athletic Stadium in Bambolim is the first integrated athletic complex in Goa. It has been constructed according to the IAAF specifications and norms and has hosted the athletic events of 2014 Lusofonia Games.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Basilica of Bom Jesus & around

items.note: The Basilica of Bom Jesus (Portuguese: Basílica do Bom Jesus; Goan Konkani: Borea Jezuchi Bajilika) is a Catholic basilica in Goa, in the Konkan region of India. The church is a pilgrimage centre and recognised by UNESCO as a World Heritage Site.

The Pelourinho Novo (lit. 'New Pillory'), also known as Hath Katro Khamb (lit. 'Hand-Cutting Pillar'), is a sixteenth-century black basalt pillory in Old Goa, located in the Indian coastal state of Goa. It acted as a symbolic civic marker and a focal point for official proceedings, where authorities handled legal matters, issued formal announcements, and enforced penalties, including corporal punishment such as the whipping of offenders.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Church of St. Anne, Talaulim?
faq.a: The best time to visit Church of St. Anne, Talaulim is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Church of St. Anne, Talaulim?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Goa International Airport, Dabolim (13 km) and the nearest railway station is Vasco da Gama (Goa) (12 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Church of St. Anne, Talaulim?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Church of St. Anne, Talaulim at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Church of St. Anne, Talaulim?
faq.a: Stays in Church of St. Anne, Talaulim start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Church of St. Anne, Talaulim?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chimbel, Kendriya Vidyalaya Bambolim, Church of St. Augustine, Goa, Museum of Christian Art, Batim.
